claim_under_attack: When definitions conflict, the most restrictive (lowest-count, most behaviorally specific) definition should be the provisional default, because permissive definitions delay detection of churn and produce optimism that does not survive contact with revenue outcomes.
evidence_or_reason: This claim asserts a default selection rule without visible evidence that restrictiveness is the right proxy for north-star validity in this case. A lower-count definition can understate healthy usage if it excludes meaningful recurring behaviors, newer product modes, or multi-user account structures. The artifact itself says the intended purpose of the metric is still unresolved and retention correlation is unknown; under those conditions, privileging the most restrictive definition is not defended by the case packet and may simply substitute one arbitrary bias for another.
